gloss_en: How are you today? Are you well?
notes: išḥālak Hassaniya greeting (tainecheikh2007 ch.10); labās (Maghrebi)

sentence: اَلْجَوّ حَامِي الْيُوم وَالرّمَل يِحْرِق فِي الصَّحْرَا.
raw: الجو حامي اليوم والرمل يحرق في الصحرا.
ipa: alˈʒaww ˈħaːmiː lˈjuːm ˈwarrmal ˈjiħriɡ ˈviː sˤˈsˤɑħraː
gloss_en: The weather is scorching today and the sand burns in the desert.
notes: Saharan semantic frame (Hassaniya context); ج=/ʒ/; sun assimilation ar-r; emphatic ص; ǧīm→[ʒ] (Ḥassāniyya) per (tainecheikh2007)

sentence: مَا نَبْغِيش قَهْوَة، نَبْغِي أَتَاي بِالنَّعْنَاع.
raw: ما نبغيش قهوة، نبغي أتاي بالنعناع.
ipa: maː nabˈʁiːʃ ˈɡahwa ˈnabʁiː ʔaˈtaːj binnaʕˈnaːʕ
gloss_en: I don't want coffee, I want tea with mint.
notes: nabɣi + ma-...-š (Maghrebi frame, tainecheikh2007); atāy (shared with Darija; Saharan tea culture)

sentence: اِشْتَرَيت ثْلَاثَة كِيلُو لْحَم مِن السُّوق بِأَلْف أُوقِيَّة.
raw: اشتريت ثلاثة كيلو لحم من السوق بألف أوقية.
ipa: ʔiʃtaˈrajt ˈθlaːθa ˈkiːluː ˈlħam min asˈsuːɡ biˈʔalv ʔuːˈɡijja
gloss_en: I bought three kilos of meat from the market for a thousand ouguiya.
notes: ث retained /θ/ θlāθa (tainecheikh2007: Hassaniya interdental retention); ūgiyya Mauritanian currency; cluster lḥ-; C9 blind-judge consensus: Ḥassāniyya drops the glottal stops (b-alf contraction, uːɡijja), article vowel ə, final -u short; the set's f→[v] kept (tainecheikh2007; both judges independently) — gold ipa corrected on the HF set; this row's ipa stays engine-pinned and the delta is an engine lead

sentence: هَاذَاكَ الرَّاجِل يِذْكُر الظُّهْر ذَاك فِي الْخَيمَة.
raw: هاذاك الراجل يذكر الظهر ذاك في الخيمة.
ipa: haːˈðaːka rˈraːʒil ˈjiðkur ɑðˤˈðˤuhr ˈðaːk ˈviː lˈxajma
gloss_en: That man remembers that noon in the tent.
notes: ذ retained /ð/ hāðāk/ðāk (Hassaniya conservatism, tainecheikh2007); ظ=/ðˤ/ first-alt; xayma 'tent'

gloss_en: Give me hot tea, boy.
notes: Hassaniyya imperative aʕtini with the atay tea word; wald with the short-vowel pattern (tainecheikh2007)

sentence: اَلْجَوّ مَاهُو بَارِد الْيُوم، هُوَ حَامِي.
raw: الجو ماهو بارد اليوم، هو حامي.
ipa: alˈʒaww ˈmaːhuː ˈbaːrid alˈjuːm ˈhuwa ˈħaːmiː
gloss_en: The weather isn't cold today, it's hot.
notes: mā-hu negative copula (Ḥassāniyya, tainecheikh2007); ج=/ʒ/ (tainecheikh2007 §2.1.1.3); ḥāmi 'hot'

gloss_en: How much is the price of this? Two hundred ouguiya.
notes: šḥāl 'how much' (Maghrebi); ث retained /θ/ θaman (tainecheikh2007 interdental retention §2.1.1.2); ūgiyya Mauritanian currency (heath2004hass); mītēn '200'

gloss_en: The appointment is at ten in the morning.
notes: fī→[viː] *f voiced [v] (tainecheikh2007 §2.1.1.3); sun assimilation ṣ-ṣbāḥ; ʕašra 'ten'

gloss_en: My family lives in that neighbourhood.
notes: ahl-i 'my family'; ذ retained /ð/ in demonstrative ðāk (tainecheikh2007 conservatism §2.1.1.2); fī→[v]

sentence: اِمْشِ نِيشَان مِنْ بَعْد دُور عَ الْيَمِين.
raw: امش نيشان من بعد دور ع اليمين.
ipa: ˈʔimʃi niːˈʃaːn min ˈbaʕd ˈduːr ʕa ljaˈmiːn
gloss_en: Go straight, then turn to the right.
notes: Saharan direction frame; imperatives imši/dūr; proclitic ʕa+article elision written as two tokens; Ḥassāniyya vowel/syncope phonology per (tainecheikh2007)

gloss_en: The white couscous was very tasty.
notes: ض→[ðˤ] abyaḍ merging with ẓāʾ (tainecheikh2007 §2.1.1.2); bnīn 'tasty' (Maghrebi); yāsir 'very much' (Bedouin/Ḥassāniyya)

gloss_en: Where's the market? I don't see it.
notes: mnēn 'where/whence' (Bedouin); ق→[ɡ] sūg (tainecheikh2007 §2.1.1.2); nšūf→[nʃuːv] *f voiced [v]; mā preverbal negation; C9 arbitrated: article vowel ə toward the judges; gold's [v] for f and suffixal -uh kept against judge 1, and the preserved diphthong mnajn kept against judge 2 (tainecheikh2007) — gold ipa corrected on the HF set; this row's ipa stays engine-pinned and the delta is an engine lead

gloss_en: How many children do you have?
notes: kam 'how many'; ṭfəl 'boy/child' with *f→[v] intervocalic pattern (tainecheikh2007 §2.1.5 ṭfəl~ṭəvl-u); ʕandak 'you have'

gloss_en: If it rains, we won't travel.
notes: ila 'if'; šta 'rain' (Maghrebi); nsāfru→[nsaːvruː] *f voiced [v] (tainecheikh2007 §2.1.1.3); ma-...-š negation

sentence: مْشِيت لِلسُّوق وَشْرِيت اَتَاي وَ لْحَم.
raw: مشيت للسوق وشريت اتاي و لحم.
ipa: ˈmʃiːt lisˈsuːɡ waʃˈriːt aˈtaːj ˈwa ˈlħam
gloss_en: I went to the market and bought tea and meat.
notes: mšīt/šrīt 1sg perfect (Maghrebi); ق→[ɡ] sūg; atāy 'tea' (Saharan tea culture); lḥam 'meat' cluster; qāf→[g] (Ḥassāniyya) per (tainecheikh2007)

gloss_en: Tomorrow I'm going to travel to the city.
notes: غ→[ʁ] ġudwa 'tomorrow', ġādi 'going-to' future marker (tainecheikh2007; ġ/q merger §2.1.1.1); namši 1sg

sentence: اِضْرَب لِي التِّلِيفُون اللِّيلَة، اَنَ نْكُون فِي الدَّار.
raw: اضرب لي التليفون الليلة، ان نكون في الدار.
ipa: ˈʔiðˤrab liː ttiliˈfuːn alˈliːla ˈana ˈnkuːn ˈviː dˈdaːr
gloss_en: Call me tonight, I'll be at home.
notes: iḍrab-li t-tilifūn 'call me'; ض→[ðˤ] iḍrab (tainecheikh2007 §2.1.1.2); fī→[v]; sun assim d-dār; nkūn 1sg

gloss_en: Peace be upon you — and upon you be peace.
notes: greeting adjacency pair; ay preserved ʕléykum (tainecheikh2007 diphthongs §2.1.3); sun assim s-salām; C9 arbitrated for the gold: the greeting is a fixed Classical formula, so as-salāmu keeps its liaison -u and a-vowel article; the judges dialectized a formula the literature treats as frozen (tainecheikh2007)

sentence: اَللِّي مَا عَنْدُه صْبَر مَا يَاكُل التّمَر.
raw: اللي ما عنده صبر ما ياكل التمر.
ipa: ˈalliː maː ˈʕanduh ˈsˤbar maː ˈjaːkul ˈattmar
gloss_en: He who has no patience doesn't eat dates.
notes: Saharan proverb frame; əlli relative, mā preverbal negation, sun-assimilation t-tamr; Ḥassāniyya consonant/vowel phonology per (tainecheikh2007)
